name: Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón
name_original: Cerro de San Pedro desde Alto del Mojón
region_intro: Jogging around Soto Del Real offers access to the diverse natural environment at the foothills of Spain's Sierra de Guadarrama. The region features varied terrain, from dehesa pastureland to Pyrenean oak and juniper forests. Runners can find routes alongside the Manzanares River and streams like the Chozas stream, with many trails offering views of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The landscape includes ancient ash trees and diverse tree cover, providing shaded running routes. Best jogging routes around Soto Del Real The most popular jogging route is Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local runners is El Hueco de San Blas loop from Canto del Berrueco, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This extensive route explores the varied terrain of the Hueco de San Blas area, including significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Soto del Real Cycling Area – Stork Nests Near Soto del Real loop from Soto del Real, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through open areas, often completed in about 58 minutes. Jogging around Soto Del Real is defined by mountainous backdrops, river paths, and diverse woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation. The routes in Soto Del Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 55 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Soto Del Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Soto Del Real?
A: Soto Del Real offers a diverse network of nearly 30 running routes. These trails cater to various preferences, from moderate paths through dehesa pastureland to more challenging mountain trails within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there challenging running trails in Soto Del Real for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Soto Del Real is known for its challenging terrain. Many routes feature significant elevation gain, particularly those venturing into the Sierra de Guadarrama. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1172441123/cerro-de-san-pedro-from-alto-del-mojon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón&lt;/a&gt; route includes over 345 meters of ascent, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19814458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hueco de San Blas loop from Canto del Berrueco&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 24 kilometers with more than 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic views in the region?
A: Absolutely. Many running routes in Soto Del Real offer stunning vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama, including landmarks like the Hoyo de Manzanares range, La Pedriza, and Cuerda Larga. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1172441123/cerro-de-san-pedro-from-alto-del-mojon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent panoramic views, while others explore the diverse landscapes of the Hueco de San Blas area, offering glimpses of La Pedriza.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easier jogging options in Soto Del Real?
A: While many routes are challenging, Soto Del Real also offers family-oriented routes with easy access and comfortable gradients. The municipality promotes urban walks and pleasant strolls ideal for stretching your legs without major climbs. The historic Cañada Real Segoviana also provides a straightforward dirt track suitable for a more relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of natural features will I encounter on the running trails?
A: You'll experience a rich natural environment. Routes often follow the Manzanares River or streams like the Chozas stream. You'll run through dehesa pastureland with ancient ash trees, Pyrenean oak and juniper forests, and diverse tree cover including pines and Montpellier maple, providing pleasant shade. Some trails also feature broom scrub in higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Soto Del Real?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Soto Del Real are loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19814458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hueco de San Blas loop from Canto del Berrueco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14883901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto del Real Cycling Area – Stork Nests Near Soto del Real loop from Soto del Real&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9 km trail.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Soto Del Real?
A: The running routes in Soto Del Real are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 55 reviews. Runners often praise the mountainous backdrops, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to run alongside rivers and through diverse woodlands.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several interesting points. You might encounter unique rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/565721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Elefantito rock formation&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de los Pastores&lt;/a&gt;. The Cañada Real Segoviana trail is also known for potential wildlife sightings, including storks and amphibians in ponds.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Soto Del Real?
A: Soto Del Real's diverse landscape makes it suitable for running across seasons. The varied tree cover provides shade during warmer months, while the lower dehesa pastureland offers pleasant conditions in cooler weather. The region's natural beauty, from river paths to mountainous backdrops, can be enjoyed year-round, though conditions will vary with altitude and season.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features?
A: Yes, many routes in Soto Del Real incorporate water features. You can find trails that run alongside the Manzanares River and various streams, such as the Chozas stream. Some trails, particularly around areas like Hoyo Cerrado and El Hueco de San Blas, even involve crossing streams multiple times, adding to the natural experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Soto Del Real?
A: Soto Del Real is a popular destination for outdoor activities, and many trails are suitable for dogs. Given the natural environment within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always check local regulations for specific areas.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Santillana Reservoir?
A: Yes, there is a route that provides views of the Santillana Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31536245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Lake – Santillana Reservoir Trail loop from Soto del Real&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.2 km route that offers scenic perspectives of the reservoir.
tips: Great view from 1423 meters. A summit book is waiting at the top.
The ascent can be mastered with individual tips, but with appropriate shoes
